news.title: Fed Balance Sheet Edges Higher to $6.694 Trillion, Signaling Liquidity Shift
news.desc: The U.S. Federal Reserve's balance sheet rose to $6.694 trillion as of April 9, 2026 (up from $6.675T), reversing recent QT-era contraction and pointing to a modest re-expansion of system liquidity. H.4.1 reserve factors data published the same day confirms the uptick.

news.title: Federal Reserve Formally Ends Quantitative Tightening to Stabilize Market Liquidity
news.desc: With the ON RRP facility hitting effectively zero in early January 2026, the Fed officially concluded its QT programme to prevent further drains on bank reserves. The move marks a structural pivot: any additional balance-sheet reduction would now come directly at the expense of reserve buffers rather than the RRP cushion.

news.title: 设定TGA上限？沃什
政策组合引发流动性讨论
news.desc: 美联储主席提名人凯文·沃什主张限制财政部一般账户（TGA）规模，以配合
政策组合。分析人士指出，若TGA被设定上限，财政部消费TGA余额将持续向银行体系净注入准备金，短期内对美元流动性构成利好，但财政部实际接受度存疑。
